title: Medical Imaging AI
short: Developing deep learning methods for detection, segmentation, and diagnosis in radiology—with a focus on data efficiency and clinical reliability.
description: We build AI models for medical image analysis that are designed to work under real-world clinical constraints: limited labeled data, domain shift, and the need for interpretable outputs. Our work includes foundation models, weakly-supervised learning, and multi-scale architectures for CT and other modalities.

title: Virtual Imaging Trials
short: Physics-based simulation of imaging pipelines—from digital patients to virtual scanners—enabling large-scale evaluation without radiation or patient risk.
description: We develop end-to-end virtual imaging trial (VIT) frameworks that computationally simulate patient anatomy, CT acquisition physics, and image interpretation. This enables rigorous protocol evaluation and AI benchmarking at a scale not possible with real clinical trials.

title: Data-Centric AI
short: Improving AI through better data—synthetic generation, quality-aware curation, and rigorous benchmarking for medical imaging.
description: Rather than only optimizing models, we focus on the data itself: generating high-fidelity synthetic images, building standardized benchmarks, and developing evaluation frameworks that connect data quality to downstream clinical performance.

title: Synthetic & Generative Imaging
short: Creating realistic synthetic medical images with controllable anatomy and pathology to augment training data and protect patient privacy.
description: Using diffusion models and physics-guided generative approaches, we synthesize medical images with precise control over anatomy, pathology, and acquisition parameters. This addresses data scarcity while preserving patient privacy and enabling reproducible research.
